untranslatable_reason: Signal requires custom external data not in allowed indicators: (1) IPO date identification and proximity check (requires IPO_DATE field), (2) Ticker phonetic simplicity scoring (requires PHONETIC_SIMPLICITY_SCORE field with linguistic processing), (3) Company name linguistic fluency metrics (requires LINGUISTIC_FLUENCY_SCORE field). These cognitive/behavioral factors cannot be derived from price/volume/technical indicators alone. The core entry logic depends on IPO recency (0-3 days post-IPO) and linguistic properties of ticker/company name, which are external fundamental data points not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This signal is fundamentally based on behavioral/linguistic factors (cognitive ease from name pronounceability and familiarity) rather than technical indicators. While we can structure the exit conditions using returns (exit at +2.5% or day 3), the entry conditions require: (1) IPO date tracking within 3-day window, (2) quantified phonetic simplicity score (>75th percentile, 3 syllables max, no consonant clusters), and (3) linguistic fluency metrics. None of these can be constructed from the allowed technical indicator set. A proper implementation would require a custom preprocessor layer that ingests IPO calendars and linguistic scoring models before price data enters the backtest engine.
